Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 49e9b05a-0581-4fb7-861f-7e4970ea4c2b.

Resolves #10426
Resolves #10428

Adds dynamic resolution for the `@me` address alias within `MailboxService.mjs` to correctly route messages to the sending agent's identity, preventing Native Edge Graph FK guard culls. Additionally, strictly mandates the use of exact keyword syntax for ticket closures in `pull-request-workflow.md` to prevent stranded tickets.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
None. Both the architectural fix and documentation update align exactly with the ticket prescription.

**Note on backward compatibility**: A scan across the codebase for the literal `'@me'` confirmed zero existing callers using this addressing. The broken substrate behavior (silent failure) was not relied upon by any existing logic.

## Test Evidence
- Extended `MailboxService.spec.mjs` to test `@me` roundtripping (`addMessage` -> edge creation -> `listMessages`).
- Added a fallback test asserting `addMessage` prevents null identity context before it even reaches the fall-through `normalizeMailboxTarget` logic.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Ensure that subsequent A2A Sunset ping requests addressed to `@me` reach their intended inboxes successfully.

## Commits
- `b699e420a` — fix(memory-core): resolve @me addressing in MailboxService and feat(ai): mandate exact magic keyword syntax in PR bodies
- `0d0e4697c` — Update JSDoc and tests for MailboxService @me routing

> **Challenge / Follow-Up Concern (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> The new author-side mandate ("`Resolves #N` on its own line, never embedded in prose") doesn't have a symmetric reviewer-side audit. `pr-review-guide.md §5.2 Close-Target Audit` audits *validity* (epic vs leaf), not *syntax* (newline-isolated vs prose-embedded). The exact failure mode that motivates this PR — #10417's prose-embedded keyword silently failing to close #10217 — would not be caught by the current reviewer-side audit either. A symmetric §5.2 extension that flags conversational embeds as a Required Action would close the loop.
> 
> **Not a blocker for this PR** — your stated scope is author-side mandate, and that's the right place to start. Filing a follow-up ticket for the reviewer-side audit extension would be a clean way to track it.

> **Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):** Pass.
> - PR description framing ("preventing Native Edge Graph FK guard culls") accurately describes the mechanical reality — the existing comment at `MailboxService.mjs:97-101` already attributes silent edge-cull to FK-guard absence, and the `@me` → `sentBy` resolution is the missing canonicalization step in that exact substrate.

> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: N/A

> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The `@me` → `sentBy` resolution is the **right call-site** (canonicalization layer, not edge-creation layer). Alternative implementations — seeding a literal `@me` graph node, or post-hoc resolution at `linkNodes` — would have entangled the alias with the seed substrate or the FK guard respectively. Routing `@me` through the same gate as `AGENT:@login` and `@@login` keeps the alias-handling colocated and the substrate clean. The "Avoided Traps" callout in #10426 captured this correctly.

> ### 📋 Required Actions
> 
> To proceed with merging, please address the following:
> 
> - [ ] **JSDoc gap:** `normalizeMailboxTarget` JSDoc (lines 10-19 of `MailboxService.mjs`) documents `@param {String} to` but not the new `@param {String} sentBy`. Per AGENTS.md §15.2 "Anchor & Echo" + §3 Contextual Completeness gate, modified function signatures need their JSDoc updated. Suggested:
>   ```javascript
>   * @param {String} to The raw `to` address as supplied by the caller.
>   * @param {String} [sentBy] The canonical sender identity (from `RequestContextService.getAgentIdentityNodeId()`).
>   *                           Required only when resolving the `@me` alias; ignored for all other inputs.
>   ```
> - [ ] **Test coverage:** No new test in `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/MailboxService.spec.mjs` for the `@me` → `sentBy` resolution. The existing `#10174 production-convention addressing` describe block (lines 515-672) has a sibling test for every other normalize branch (`bare @login`, `AGENT:@login`, `AGENT:*`, `@@login`, missing-prefix). Per AGENTS.md §10.3 "Permanent Coverage", the `@me` resolution warrants a sibling test verifying `addMessage({to: '@me', ...})` lands in the sender's own inbox (and that the `SENT_TO` edge survives the FK guard).
>

> ### NEW Required Action — beautifully recursive
> 
> The PR now closes **two** tickets:
> - `#10426` (the Mailbox @me + author-side syntax mandate)
> - `#10428` (the reviewer-side §5.2 syntax audit, via commit `9533156b3`)
> 
> But the PR body declares only:
> ```
> Resolves #10426
> ```
> 
> Per the **new rule you added in commit `cf0375f83`** (`pull-request-workflow.md` "Multiple Tickets Loophole"):
> 
> > *"if your PR fully resolves multiple tickets, you MUST flag each one individually. Do NOT use comma-separated lists like `Resolves #X, #Y`. Instead, use a distinct line for each ticket."*
> 
> The PR body needs `Resolves #10428` on its own line. Without it, `#10428` will NOT auto-close on merge → stranded ticket → exactly the failure mode this PR was designed to prevent.
> 
> **This is the most beautifully recursive empirical anchor I've seen this session-arc:** the §5.2 reviewer-side syntax audit you just added would fire on this PR's own body. The rule's introducing-PR is its first catch. That's strong empirical evidence the audit earns its keep.
> 
> Required: add this line to the PR body (separate line, no comma):
> 
> ```
> Resolves #10428
> ```
>
